skill.name: smtp-penetration-testing
skill.description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "perform SMTP penetration testing", "enumerate email users", "test for open mail relays", "grab SMTP banners", "brute force email cre...
skill.summary: Comprehensive SMTP server security assessment skill covering banner grabbing, user enumeration, open relay testing, and TLS configuration analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill teaches legitimate SMTP penetration testing techniques using standard security tools (Nmap, Metasploit, Hydra). Static scanner flagged 181 potential issues including Metasploit usage, network scanning tools, and brute force commands. However, these are FALSE POSITIVES - the flagged patterns are standard penetration testing tools and techniques used by security professionals for authorized assessments. The skill includes legal disclaimers requiring written authorization. Risk level set to HIGH because the skill provides actionable instructions for user enumeration, brute force attacks, and relay testing that could be misused without proper authorization.

high_findings.title: Metasploit Framework Usage
high_findings.description: Skill teaches use of Metasploit auxiliary modules for SMTP enumeration and relay testing. Metasploit is a legitimate penetration testing framework commonly used by security professionals for authorized assessments.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Metasploit is explicitly mentioned with specific module names (auxiliary/scanner/smtp/smtp_enum, smtp_login, smtp_relay). This is a standard tool in the penetration testing community.

high_findings.title: Brute Force Authentication Testing
high_findings.description: Skill teaches brute force attacks against SMTP authentication using Hydra and Medusa tools. These are standard password cracking tools used in authorized penetration tests.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hydra commands with password wordlists are explicitly documented. This is a common technique for testing weak credentials but could be used maliciously.

medium_findings.title: User Enumeration Techniques
medium_findings.description: Skill documents VRFY, EXPN, and RCPT command enumeration methods to discover valid email addresses. These are standard reconnaissance techniques.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: User enumeration is a well-known SMTP vulnerability test. The skill uses standard tools (smtp-user-enum) for this purpose.

medium_findings.title: Open Relay Testing
medium_findings.description: Skill teaches testing for open mail relays which can be exploited for spam distribution. Testing for open relays is a legitimate security assessment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Open relay testing is a standard SMTP security assessment. The skill documents both manual and automated testing methods.

low_findings.title: Network Scanning Tools
low_findings.description: Skill uses Nmap for service discovery and vulnerability scanning. Nmap is a standard network reconnaissance tool used by security professionals.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Nmap is a universally accepted security testing tool. Flagged as 'network scanning' which is standard practice for legitimate assessments.

low_findings.title: Banner Grabbing Documentation
low_findings.description: Skill documents banner grabbing techniques using Telnet, Netcat, and Nmap. Banner grabbing is basic reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Banner grabbing is fundamental to network security testing and is not inherently malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Actionable Attack Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: Skill provides step-by-step instructions for user enumeration, brute force attacks, and open relay testing without technical guardrails preventing misuse.

content.value_statement: This skill enables security professionals to conduct comprehensive SMTP server penetration tests, identifying vulnerabilities like open relays, weak authentication, and user enumeration risks.
actual_capabilities: Perform SMTP banner grabbing to identify server software and versions
Execute user enumeration using VRFY, EXPN, and RCPT commands
Test for open mail relay vulnerabilities
Conduct brute force authentication testing against SMTP servers
Analyze TLS/SSL configuration and check for weak ciphers
Verify SPF, DKIM, and DMARC email authentication implementations
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization before testing any target system
VRFY and EXPN commands are often disabled on modern secure SMTP servers
Rate limiting and account lockout policies may impede testing
Cannot test internal mail systems without network access

use_cases.title: Authorized Security Assessment
use_cases.description: Security professionals conducting penetration tests on organization-owned mail servers to identify and remediate vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Email Infrastructure Hardening
use_cases.description: System administrators evaluating their SMTP server configurations to ensure compliance with security best practices.

use_cases.title: Security Training and Education
use_cases.description: Security training environments teaching students about SMTP vulnerabilities and testing methodologies in controlled lab settings.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SMTP Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform an SMTP penetration test on mail.example.com. Identify the SMTP server version, test for open relay vulnerabilities, and check if VRFY/EXPN commands are enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: A basic SMTP security assessment on a single target

prompt_templates.title: User Enumeration Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct user enumeration testing against the SMTP server at 192.168.1.50 using the VRFY and RCPT methods. Use the provided wordlist for testing common usernames.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing for valid email addresses through enumeration

prompt_templates.title: Full Security Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a comprehensive SMTP security assessment including banner grabbing, user enumeration, open relay testing, TLS configuration analysis, and SPF/DKIM/DMARC verification for domain example.com.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete SMTP security audit with multiple testing phases

prompt_templates.title: Brute Force Authentication Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Test SMTP authentication security on mail.target.com using Hydra with the top-100 password wordlist. Report any weak credentials discovered.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing for weak or default SMTP credentials

output_examples.input: Perform SMTP banner grab on mail.target.com
output_examples.output:
Banner Analysis Results:
- Server: Postfix
- Version: 3.4.5
- Hostname: mail.target.com
- Supported Extensions: PIPELINING, SIZE, VRFY, ETRN, STARTTLS, AUTH PLAIN LOGIN
- Security Concerns: VRFY command is enabled (user enumeration risk)

output_examples.input: Test for open relay on mail.target.com
output_examples.output:
Open Relay Test Results:
- Test 1 (anonymous): PASS (vulnerable)
- Test 2 (authenticated): BLOCKED
- Recommendation: Disable anonymous relay; require authentication for external delivery

best_practices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any system you do not own
Document all testing activities including timestamps, commands executed, and findings
Use rate limiting in your testing to avoid overwhelming target systems
Report vulnerabilities to system administrators through proper channels
anti_patterns: Never test systems without explicit authorization, even for educational purposes
Avoid using real passwords or sensitive data during testing
Do not exploit discovered vulnerabilities beyond the scope of authorized testing
Never share or sell harvested email addresses or credentials

faq.question: Is SMTP penetration testing legal?
faq.answer: SMTP penetration testing is legal only when performed on systems you own or have explicit written authorization to test. Unauthorized testing is illegal and may result in criminal charges.

faq.question: What tools are required for SMTP testing?
faq.answer: Common tools include Nmap with SMTP scripts, smtp-user-enum, Hydra for brute force, Netcat for manual testing, and Metasploit for advanced modules.

faq.question: Can I test any SMTP server I find?
faq.answer: No. Finding an SMTP server does not give you permission to test it. You must have written authorization from the system owner before conducting any security testing.

faq.question: What are the main SMTP vulnerabilities to test?
faq.answer: Key vulnerabilities include open relay misconfigurations, enabled VRFY/EXPN commands (user enumeration), weak authentication, missing or weak TLS encryption, and missing email authentication records (SPF/DKIM/DMARC).

faq.question: How do I test for open relays safely?
faq.answer: Use Nmap's smtp-open-relay script or manual testing with test@example.com as the external recipient. Always use test domains and document findings without actually relaying spam.

faq.question: What should I do if I find a critical vulnerability?
faq.answer: Document the finding thoroughly, immediately stop any further testing that could cause damage, and report the vulnerability to the system administrator through proper channels following responsible disclosure practices.
